text: I view hotels merely as a place to sleep, so I look for the cheapest clean option.
A perfect day involves lounging by the resort pool and ordering room service.
I prefer staying in hostels or guesthouses where I can easily meet other travelers.
When choosing accommodation, my top priority is whether it's near trendy cafes or restaurants.
I love exploring the local nightlife, from bustling night markets to lively pubs.
I often use taxis or rideshare apps (like Uber) to save time between destinations.
Navigating local buses or subway systems is an enjoyable part of the adventure for me.
I prefer renting a car to drive myself, especially on scenic routes.
I feel most at ease when I have a detailed itinerary planned down to the hour.
I enjoy wandering without a destination and stumbling upon hidden alleyways or shops.
I start my day very early to beat the crowds and see as much as possible.
I like to book a one-way ticket and figure out accommodation and plans after I arrive.
I prefer sitting quietly by the ocean or in a forest over visiting crowded tourist spots.
Visiting museums, art galleries, and historical sites is a mandatory part of my trips.
My itinerary isn't complete without time set aside for shopping at malls or outlets.
I seek out remote trekking trails or off-the-beaten-path adventures.
I don't mind waiting in line to get a photo at a famous landmark.
I focus on soaking in the moment with my own eyes rather than taking photos.
I prefer bargaining at local markets and eating street food over fine dining.
I reserve tables at top-rated or Michelin-star restaurants weeks in advance.
I feel a true sense of freedom and focus when I explore a new place entirely on my own.
Beautiful scenery or delicious food feels wasted if I don't have someone to share it with.
I look forward to drinking and socializing with locals or other travelers.
I find joy in navigating language barriers using body language, even if it's challenging.
I prioritize wearing outfits that look good in photos over purely comfortable clothes.
I am willing to wake up at 4 AM to hike a mountain for the sunrise.
I save 'hot places' seen on Instagram or YouTube to my map and make sure to visit them.
I meticulously check my shopping list for the duty-free shop before heading to the airport.
Even on a beach vacation, I stay busy with lessons like surfing or scuba diving.
There is a romance in bringing camping gear, pitching a tent, and cooking my own meals.
I tolerate long layovers or multiple transfers if it saves money on airfare.
I enjoy joining guided tours or docent-led programs to learn deeply about a place.
Sleeping in late and having a leisurely brunch is my definition of a true vacation.
I pack very lightly and prefer to buy whatever I need locally.
I prefer visiting a new country or city I've never been to over returning to a familiar favorite.
If the weather is bad, I am happy to spend the day reading in the hotel lobby or a cafe.
I record video footage of almost everything to create a vlog or travel reel.
I trust a restaurant recommendation from a local resident more than online reviews.
I love the atmosphere of sipping a cocktail at a rooftop bar with a view.
I enjoy staring out the window at the passing scenery while moving between cities.
I am willing to run at the opening time ('open run') to get into popular theme parks.
Breakfast is essential, so I always make sure it's included in my accommodation booking.
I often keep in touch with friends I met during my travels even after returning home.
I am willing to carry heavy camera equipment to get the highest quality photos.
When booking a hotel, I carefully check amenities like the gym and swimming pool.
I love the thrill of hopping on a train to a nearby town on a whim.
I am more attracted to the rustic charm of the countryside than the glamour of big cities.
I occasionally check work emails or open my laptop during my trip.
I try to learn at least a few phrases of the local language to use during my stay.
On the last day, I spend all my remaining cash on souvenirs or gifts.
